flavorText: Valor Bards inspire from the front lines, blending martial prowess and heroic performance.

features.description: Gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.
A creature that has your Bardic Inspiration can use the die to increase weapon damage or as a reaction to increase AC against one attack.
You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
When you cast a bard spell using your action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

valor-bonus-proficiencies.name: Bonus Proficiencies
valor-bonus-proficiencies.summary: This subclass grants extra training so your sheet reflects a broader skill, tool, or weapon identity than the base class alone.
valor-bonus-proficiencies.description:
Gain proficiency with medium armor, shields, and martial weapons.

Bonus Proficiencies is not glamorous on its own, but it changes what the character can reliably contribute. The important thing is surfacing the added proficiencies clearly so they affect real rolls.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.

valor-combat-inspiration.name: Combat Inspiration
valor-combat-inspiration.summary: Your inspiration now helps allies fight, not just pass checks and saves.
valor-combat-inspiration.description:
A creature that has your Bardic Inspiration can use the die to increase weapon damage or as a reaction to increase AC against one attack.

Combat Inspiration makes Valor bard support more martial. It matters because the bard can turn inspiration into direct attack or defense swing value instead of purely out-of-combat utility.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.

valor-extra-attack.name: Extra Attack
valor-extra-attack.summary: When you take the Attack action, you can now make up to 2 attacks within that action.
valor-extra-attack.description:
You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

This progression point changes your basic turn structure by increasing how many attacks fit inside a normal Attack action. It should be reflected everywhere the sheet explains attack cadence.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.

valor-battle-magic.name: Battle Magic
valor-battle-magic.summary: Casting and striking start linking together so your turn can mix spell pressure with weapon follow-through.
valor-battle-magic.description:
When you cast a bard spell using your action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

Battle Magic should feel like a genuine action-economy payoff. It matters most when the bard wants to keep spell presence without giving up on weapon tempo entirely.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.
valor-battle-magic.trigger: When you cast a bard spell using your action
